docs.title: Super Individuals CIC
docs.text:
Public-Interest AI Community

Build Your Personal AI System.

Super Individuals CIC is a community for people who do not just learn AI. They can weave AI, tools, knowledge, and processes into their own system.

Apply for Next Workshop Join the Community

docs.title: What Super Individuals Is
docs.text:
Build, not browse

We are building a community of individuals who do not just study AI from the sidelines. They use it to build, test, ship, and learn through execution.

About Super Individuals Domain experts in action

We help people from different fields turn their own expertise into domain-specific agents, workflows, and practical outcomes.

Community and membership Curated by participation

No passive learning. No endless theory. No spectators. This is a place for people who are willing to build.

See the workshop path

docs.title: What Super Individuals CIC Is
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC is a workshop and membership community focused on building with AI.

We bring together professionals, builders, and curious minds to explore and apply AI in real-world scenarios, then give them a longer-term community in which to keep building.

docs.title: What We Do
docs.text:
• workshops
  • cohort-based learning
  • invitation-only membership
  • project building

docs.title: Our Goal
docs.text:
To help individuals become AI-native builders, not just consumers of knowledge.

We do that through a workshop gateway, followed by a deeper membership layer for people who are invited to continue.

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
Super Individuals is a long-term community built around one idea: that the most valuable thing AI can do is make it possible for individuals, not just institutions, to create and deliver things that genuinely matter.

We are building a curated, invitation-only ecosystem for people who want to keep building with AI over time, not just attend a single workshop and disappear.

docs.title: Membership First
docs.text:
Membership is the long-term layer of Super Individuals. It is designed for people who want an ongoing place to learn, build, stay close to frontier practice, and grow alongside other serious builders across cohorts.

Membership includes:

  • Discord community access as the permanent home for members
  • all future workshops, free, across cohorts and streams
  • Substack member content including research notes, build logs, and internal thinking
  • free Q&A sessions with the founding team and guest practitioners
  • weekly or fortnightly frontier updates on what is actually moving in applied AI
  • member directory access across cohorts
  • product and framework beta access before public release
  • co-facilitator opportunities for members who want to contribute
  • early pathways into Pegasi and Tensorlink opportunities where relevant

docs.title: The Founding Cohort
docs.text:
The Founding Cohort is the first membership group inside Super Individuals. These are the earliest members of the community, recorded permanently as part of its origin.

Founding Cohort membership is:

  • limited to 31 members
  • invitation only
  • lifetime access
  • the lowest membership price this community will ever offer
  • help shape the culture and direction of the community

As the community grows, the founding rate rises and later members join at higher pricing. This is a structural commitment to the people who were here first.

docs.title: Founding-Only Benefits
docs.text:
What founding members get on top
  • Permanent founding member status on record
  • Special founding cohort badge in Discord
  • Private founding-member channel in Discord
  • Closer access to shaping early community culture and direction
  • Priority visibility for future co-facilitator and contribution opportunities
Why it matters
  • Your place remains marked even as the community scales
  • You are part of the first layer of trust inside the network
  • You get earlier access to conversations, experiments, and programme evolution
  • You help establish the tone for everyone who comes after

docs.title: Membership Pricing
docs.text:
First membership tier, 31 seats only

Founding Cohort

GBP 49.99 lifetime

Invitation-only founding membership with additional founder status and Discord benefits.

  • Free access to all future workshops
  • Full member access across the community
  • Special founding cohort badge
  • Private founding Discord channel
  • Permanent founding member status
  • Substack research notes, build logs, and private thinking
  • Q&A sessions, frontier briefings, and beta access
  • Early pathways into Pegasi and Tensorlink opportunities
Invitation Only

Future membership tiers

Coming soon

Additional membership tiers will be announced later as the community grows beyond the founding cohort.

For now, only the Founding Cohort membership price is being offered.

Coming soon

docs.title: How Membership Works
docs.text:
Membership is not open for public payment. It is offered only after a participant completes and passes the Intro Batch workshop, followed by a final review of results.

If invited, the participant receives a private payment link by email.

The Founding Cohort is available only to selected participants from the earliest workshop cohorts, until all 31 places are filled.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For collaborations, partnerships, or questions:

docs.title: Contact Routes
docs.text:
• publication@sicic.org

We are open to:

  • academic collaborations
  • industry partnerships
  • guest speakers

docs.title: Partnership Direction
docs.text: Super Individuals CIC is designed to work with institutions, communities, alumni networks, and mission-aligned organizations that want to help domain experts build practical, domain-specific AI capability.

docs.title: Current Context
docs.text: Our first workshop was framed in an Oxford Saïd alumni context, giving the community an early home among thoughtful professionals.

docs.title: Future Partners
docs.text:
We are open to collaborations with:

  • alumni communities
  • universities and executive education programs
  • innovation labs and public-interest organizations
  • founder networks and professional associations
  • media, research, and cultural institutions

docs.title: Partnership Formats
docs.text:
• private workshop cohorts
  • invited talks and salon events
  • co-branded learning series
  • cohort showcases and member demos
  • editorial collaborations and published reflections

docs.title: What Partners Gain
docs.text:
• a credible entry point into domain-specific AI-agent education
  • programming that feels practical rather than hype-driven
  • stronger engagement through cohort-based participation
  • visible outputs that members can share publicly

docs.title: Start a Conversation
docs.text: To explore a workshop, event, or strategic collaboration, contact publication@sicic.org.

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC ("SICIC", "we", "us", "our")

Last updated: April 2026

docs.title: 1. Who we are
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC is a Community Interest Company registered in England and Wales. We operate an invitation-only AI agent education community and workshop programme.

Our registered contact for data matters is publication@sicic.org.

docs.title: 2. What personal data we collect
docs.text:
We collect and process the following categories of personal data:

  • Membership and application data: name, email address, professional background, LinkedIn profile URL, and responses submitted via our membership application form.
  • Payment data: name and email address associated with payment. Payment card details are processed directly by Stripe Payments UK Ltd and are not stored by SICIC.
  • Community participation data: messages and interactions within our Telegram community channels, responses to workshop exercises, and workshop attendance records.
  • Technical data: IP addresses and usage logs generated through interaction with our technical infrastructure, including our Telegram bot (Beacon).
  • Research data: with your separate and explicit consent only, anonymised or identified content from community interactions may be used for academic research into human-AI collaboration. See Section 7.

docs.title: 3. Legal basis for processing
docs.text:
We rely on the following lawful bases under UK GDPR:

  • Contract performance (Article 6(1)(b)): processing necessary to administer your membership, deliver workshop content, and manage payments.
  • Legitimate interests (Article 6(1)(f)): operating and improving our community platform, ensuring security, and communicating with members about programme updates.
  • Consent (Article 6(1)(a)): where we ask for your agreement before processing, in particular for use of your data in academic research, or for publishing any content featuring you such as spotlights or case studies.
  • Legal obligation (Article 6(1)(c)): where processing is required to comply with UK law, including financial record-keeping.

docs.title: 4. How we use your data
docs.text:
We use your personal data to:

  • process and administer your membership application and payment
  • deliver workshop curriculum and community support
  • communicate with you about SICIC programmes and events
  • operate and maintain the safety and integrity of our community
  • comply with our legal and regulatory obligations
  • conduct internal analysis to improve our programmes

docs.title: 5. Who we share your data with
docs.text:
We do not sell your personal data. We share data only with the following categories of third-party processors, under appropriate data processing agreements:

Processor Purpose Location Stripe Payments UK Ltd Payment processing UK / EU Telegram Messenger Community platform EU (servers vary) Hetzner Online GmbH Cloud infrastructure hosting Germany (EU) OpenRouter Inc. AI model API (bot functionality) USA*

* Where data is transferred outside the UK/EEA, we rely on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or equivalent UK adequacy mechanisms.

docs.title: 6. How long we keep your data
docs.text:
• Membership records: for the duration of your membership, plus 6 years thereafter (UK legal requirement for financial records).
  • Application data for unsuccessful applicants: deleted within 12 months of the application decision.
  • Community interaction data (Telegram): retained while the community is active; members may request deletion of their contributions, subject to technical limitations of the Telegram platform.
  • Payment records: 6 years from transaction date, as required by HMRC.

docs.title: 7. Research use of community data
docs.text:
SICIC conducts and supports academic research into human-AI interaction and organisational behaviour. Community interaction data, including Telegram messages and workshop participation records, represents a longitudinal dataset of value to this research.

We will only use your data for research purposes if you provide separate, explicit, and informed consent. This consent will be sought through a dedicated consent form prior to any research project commencing, and will clearly describe what data will be used, how it will be anonymised where applicable, who will have access, and the intended publication venue.

You may withdraw your research consent at any time without affecting your membership.

docs.title: 8. Your rights
docs.text:
Under UK GDPR, you have the right to:

  • access the personal data we hold about you
  • rectify inaccurate data
  • erase your data ("right to be forgotten"), subject to our legal retention obligations
  • restrict processing in certain circumstances
  • object to processing based on legitimate interests
  • data portability for data you provided under contract or consent
  • withdraw consent at any time, without affecting prior processing

To exercise any of these rights, contact us at publication@sicic.org. We will respond within one calendar month.

docs.title: 9. Cookies and tracking
docs.text: Our primary community platform (Telegram) and payment provider (Stripe) may set cookies or use tracking technologies on their own platforms. Please refer to their respective privacy policies. SICIC does not operate its own website cookies at this time.

docs.title: 10. Changes to this policy
docs.text: We may update this policy from time to time. Where changes are material, we will notify members via our community channels with reasonable notice before the changes take effect.

docs.title: 11. How to complain
docs.text:
If you have concerns about how we handle your personal data, please contact us first at publication@sicic.org. If you remain unsatisfied, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the UK Information Commissioner's Office:

  • ico.org.uk
  • 0303 123 1113

docs.title: 1. Non-refundable nature of membership
docs.text: All SICIC membership fees are non-refundable upon payment. Membership is a lifetime access entitlement that takes effect immediately upon confirmation of payment. By completing payment, the member acknowledges that the membership service has commenced and expressly consents to the waiver of any applicable cancellation right.

docs.title: 2. Basis under UK Consumer Rights Act 2015
docs.text:
Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and the Consumer Contracts (Information, Cancellation and Additional Charges) Regulations 2013, consumers ordinarily have a 14-day right to cancel distance contracts. However, this right does not apply where:

  • the service has been fully performed
  • the consumer has given prior express consent to performance beginning before the cancellation period has expired and acknowledged that the right to cancel will be lost

By proceeding with payment, the member provides such consent and acknowledgement.

docs.title: 3. Pre-purchase evaluation
docs.text: SICIC membership is accessible only through an invitation-only process that requires prior completion of a paid workshop. Members have therefore had a meaningful opportunity to evaluate the community, curriculum, and culture before committing to membership. No refund will be issued on the basis that expectations were not met.

docs.title: 4. Exceptional circumstances
docs.text: SICIC reserves the right to consider refund requests in exceptional circumstances at its sole discretion. Such requests must be submitted in writing to publication@sicic.org within 7 days of payment. The decision of SICIC's directors shall be final.

docs.title: 5. Membership transfer
docs.text: Membership is personal and non-transferable. It may not be assigned, gifted, or sold to another individual.

docs.title: Workshop Refund Policy
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC ("SICIC")

Last updated: April 2026

docs.title: 1. General principle
docs.text: Workshop fees are non-refundable once the workshop has commenced. Prior to commencement, refund eligibility is determined by the notice period given, as set out below.

docs.title: 2. Cancellation by the participant
docs.text:
Notice given before workshop start Refund More than 7 days Full refund 3-7 days 50% refund Less than 3 days No refund After workshop has commenced No refund

Cancellation requests must be submitted in writing to publication@sicic.org. The date of receipt of the written request determines the applicable notice period.

docs.title: 3. Deferral option
docs.text: In lieu of a refund, participants who cancel with at least 3 days' notice may request to defer their place to the next available cohort of the same workshop stream. Deferral is subject to availability and may be exercised once only. Deferred places have no cash value and cannot be transferred to another individual.

docs.title: 4. Cancellation by SICIC
docs.text:
In the event that SICIC cancels a workshop before it commences, participants will receive a full refund or, at their election, a deferred place in the next available cohort. SICIC's liability in such circumstances is limited to the workshop fee paid and does not extend to any travel, accommodation, or other costs incurred by the participant.

SICIC reserves the right to reschedule a workshop with reasonable notice. Rescheduling does not entitle participants to a refund unless the new date is materially inconvenient and no suitable alternative is offered.

docs.title: 5. No-shows
docs.text: Participants who do not attend a workshop without prior written cancellation are not entitled to a refund or deferral.

docs.title: 6. Exceptional circumstances
docs.text: SICIC may consider refund requests outside the above framework in genuinely exceptional circumstances, for example serious illness or bereavement, at its sole discretion. Such requests must be submitted within 7 days of the missed workshop with supporting documentation where appropriate.

docs.title: 7. Consumer rights
docs.text: Where applicable, nothing in this policy affects your statutory rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 or the Consumer Contracts (Information, Cancellation and Additional Charges) Regulations 2013.

docs.title: Terms of Service
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC ("SICIC", "we", "us", "our")

Last updated: April 2026

docs.title: 1. About SICIC
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC is a Community Interest Company registered in England and Wales. We operate an invitation-only AI agent education programme and community ecosystem. Our mission is to support the co-evolution of humans and AI through practical, cohort-based learning.

As a Community Interest Company, SICIC is constituted for community benefit. Membership fees and any surplus revenue are reinvested into programme delivery, research, and community development. Membership does not confer any ownership interest, equity, or profit entitlement in SICIC or any associated entity.

docs.title: 2. Acceptance of Terms
docs.text:
By submitting a membership application, completing payment, or participating in any SICIC workshop or community platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service, our Privacy Policy, and our Membership Refund Policy, each of which is incorporated herein by reference.

If you do not agree to these terms, you should not proceed with membership or participation.

SICIC operates an invitation-only model. Admission requires:

  • receipt of a valid invitation or referral from an existing member or SICIC director
  • completion of at least one SICIC workshop
  • acceptance by SICIC following any applicable review process
SICIC reserves the right to decline any application without providing a reason.
Membership is personal and non-transferable. You may not share, assign, or resell your membership or any associated access credentials.
You represent that the information you provide in your application is accurate and complete. Membership obtained through misrepresentation may be revoked without refund.
Workshop access is granted upon payment of the applicable workshop fee as set out in your invitation or on our booking page at the time of registration.

Workshop content, including curriculum materials, recorded sessions, agent frameworks, and code examples, is provided for your personal educational use only. You may not redistribute, resell, publish, or sublicense any workshop content without prior written consent from SICIC.

SICIC workshops involve the use of third-party tools and platforms including but not limited to Telegram, Discord, Hetzner VPS, OpenRouter, and n8n. Your use of these platforms is governed by their respective terms of service. SICIC is not responsible for any changes, outages, or policy updates affecting these third-party services.
Workshop schedules, curriculum, and technical requirements may be updated at SICIC's discretion. We will provide reasonable notice of material changes.

SICIC operates community platforms on Telegram and Discord. Access to these platforms is a privilege of membership and may be revoked at SICIC's discretion. SICIC may migrate, consolidate, or add community platforms over time; members will be notified in advance of any such transitions and provided with reasonable onboarding support.

Members agree not to:

  • share API keys, credentials, server access details, or any security-sensitive information in community channels
  • publish, reproduce, or distribute content created by other members without their explicit consent
  • engage in harassment, discrimination, or conduct that undermines the safety or inclusivity of the community
  • use community channels for unsolicited commercial promotion
  • attempt to reverse-engineer, scrape, or systematically extract community content

Security incidents, including accidental sharing of credentials, must be reported immediately to SICIC via direct message to a director or via publication@sicic.org. Swift reporting will be treated as a positive demonstration of community responsibility.
SICIC reserves the right to remove any member from community platforms who breaches these conduct standards, with or without prior warning depending on the severity of the breach.

docs.title: 6.1 SICIC content
docs.text: All curriculum materials, workshop recordings, documentation, agent frameworks, brand assets, and other content produced by SICIC or its directors remain the intellectual property of SICIC. No licence is granted beyond personal educational use.

docs.title: 6.2 Community contributions
docs.text: Where you contribute content to community channels, discussion threads, or co-created resources, you grant SICIC a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use, reference, and reproduce that content for internal programme improvement and, with your separate consent, for research or publication purposes.

docs.title: 6.3 Feedback
docs.text: If you provide feedback, suggestions, or ideas about SICIC's programmes, you agree that SICIC may use such feedback without restriction or compensation.

SICIC provides education on the design and deployment of AI agents. We do not warrant that any technique, framework, or tool taught in our workshops will produce specific outcomes, be free from errors, or remain current as AI technology evolves.

Members are solely responsible for any agents, automations, or systems they build and deploy. SICIC accepts no liability for any direct, indirect, or consequential loss arising from the use of skills, code, or frameworks introduced in our workshops.

AI-generated content encountered during workshops, whether from large language models, agents, or automated pipelines, may contain inaccuracies. Members are responsible for independently verifying any output before acting on it in a professional or commercial context.
All fees are payable in GBP via the payment method specified at checkout, currently processed by Stripe Payments UK Ltd.
Workshop fees and membership fees are non-refundable upon payment, as further described in our Membership Refund Policy.
SICIC reserves the right to update its fee structure at any time. Changes will not affect fees already paid.

SICIC may suspend or terminate your membership if you:

  • breach these Terms of Service
  • engage in conduct that SICIC reasonably determines to be harmful to the community
  • provide false information in your application
  • fail to maintain the standard of engagement expected of members

Upon termination, your access to all community platforms including Telegram and Discord, and to workshop materials, will be revoked. Membership fees already paid will not be refunded, except at SICIC's sole discretion in exceptional circumstances.
You may voluntarily withdraw from the community at any time by notifying publication@sicic.org. Withdrawal does not entitle you to a refund.

docs.title: 10. Limitation of Liability
docs.text:
To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, SICIC's total liability to any member for any claim arising out of or in connection with these terms shall not exceed the total fees paid by that member to SICIC in the twelve months preceding the claim. SICIC shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, or reputational loss.

Nothing in these terms excludes liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, fraud, or any other liability that cannot be excluded under English law.

docs.title: 11. Governing Law and Disputes
docs.text:
These Terms of Service are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any dispute arising from or in connection with these terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

Before initiating any formal dispute, members agree to first contact SICIC at publication@sicic.org to attempt resolution in good faith.

docs.title: 12. Changes to These Terms
docs.text: SICIC may update these Terms of Service from time to time. Where changes materially affect members' rights or obligations, we will provide reasonable advance notice via our community channels before the updated terms take effect. Continued participation after that date constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

docs.title: 13. Contact
docs.text:
For any questions regarding these terms, please contact:

Super Individuals CIC publication@sicic.org

docs.title: Level-Based Workshop System
docs.text: Our workshops are organized by level, so each batch belongs to a clear track. This makes it easier for members to understand where to start, what depth to expect, and who they will be learning alongside.

docs.title: Application Flow
docs.text: Workshops are not open-pay. Applicants register first through a form, we review for fit, referrals, and profile, and selected applicants receive a private payment link by email.

docs.title: Membership Requirement
docs.text:
Completing and passing an intro-level workshop is the requirement for acquiring Super Individuals CIC membership. The workshop is the gateway. Membership is the longer-term community layer that follows.

For the first invited members, that longer-term layer is the Founding Cohort: the first 31 lifetime members, with founding-only status, a special Discord badge, and a private founding channel.

See what the first founding cohort members are already building on the Cohort page.

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
Intro Track

Batch-2

2 May - 30 May

Founding Cohort Refer 15 slots GBP 86 Open now Early Bird 20 slots GBP 139 Open Now Standard 20 slots GBP 199 Next Tier

Move from consuming AI to building with it.

  • Set up a remote AI development environment
  • Understand the structure of agent-based systems
  • Design your own workflow
  • Build and deploy your first usable AI agent
  • Leave with a working agent, repo, workflow, and live demo

Output: ship a functional agent, structure it clearly, and document it properly.

Apply Now

Builder Track

Batch-1

Starting Early June

Price and Slots Coming soon 20-30 slots Not open

Move beyond a single agent and build a structured AI system.

  • Connect and orchestrate tools, automation, memory, and workflows
  • Structure your agent for efficiency and reuse
  • Design multi-step workflows for real use cases
  • Improve cost-performance and execution quality
  • Leave with a reusable workflow system tailored to your needs

Output: build a multi-step workflow system and demonstrate real-world usability.

Register Interest

Advanced Track

Batch-1 Price and slots Coming soon Later

This is a build environment for shipping a real AI-driven project.

  • Form a small team around a shared idea
  • Define a real problem worth solving
  • Build an AI-powered product or system
  • Iterate based on feedback
  • Leave with a working prototype or product direction

Output: deliver a working prototype, define the problem clearly, and show real usage or potential.

Payment Coming Soon

docs.title: Apply
docs.text:
To request a place in the next session:

  • complete the Intro Track — Batch-2 Application Form
  • if Builder Track is open for interest, use the Builder Track — Batch-1 Interest Form
  • shortlisted applicants receive a private Stripe payment link by email

To invite a workshop for your network:

  • email publication@sicic.org

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Our cohorts focus on building real AI applications. Participants work on projects involving AI agents, automation, and practical use cases.

docs.title: Founding Cohort
docs.text:
The founding cohort is the first membership group inside Super Individuals. These early members are building in public, shipping real work, and establishing the tone for what the community becomes.

Projects are built within a limited timeframe. The focus is on execution, not perfection.

docs.text: Founding Cohort Demo

docs.title: Gemma Oldfield
docs.text:
Founding Cohort

My Digital Lab

Built on the ZeroClaw framework, this is Gemma Oldfield's space for designing, testing, and evolving agent-driven workflows that turn thinking into action.

View Live Demo

docs.title: Project Snapshot
docs.text:
Builder

Gemma Oldfield

Founding cohort member.

Type

Digital Lab

A working project space for agent architecture, workflow design, and iteration.

Lead Agent

Weeny Jeanie

An AI thought-leadership companion for turning raw thinking into structured social content.

docs.title: Why Gemma Built This
docs.text:
In the AI era, Gemma does not just want to use tools. She wants to build and own intelligent systems.

This lab is where she designs, tests, and evolves agent-driven workflows that turn thinking into action.

It documents:

  • the architecture behind her AI agents
  • the workflows that power them
  • everything that broke along the way

Because the real value is not only in what works. It is also in understanding why it did not.

docs.title: Weeny Jeanie: Thought Leadership Companion
docs.text:
Gemma's primary agent is an AI thought-leadership companion designed to turn raw, unstructured thinking into high-quality social content.

What it does:

  • captures ideas via Telegram through voice, text, image, or links
  • transforms unstructured input into clear, structured insight
  • generates multiple LinkedIn-ready post variations
  • refines tone, clarity, and positioning based on intent

docs.title: Core Capability
docs.text:
Weeny Jeanie acts as a personal thinking partner, not just a content generator.

  • identifies the underlying theme such as leadership, AI, or creativity
  • understands audience and intent
  • shapes ideas into credible, human, insight-led content
  • filters and improves thinking before it becomes output

docs.title: Design Philosophy
docs.text: Principle Meaning Human-in-the-loop Quality over automation Insight-first Not just content, but thinking Modular Designed to evolve into multi-agent systems Low-friction Capture ideas instantly, anywhere

docs.title: What This Is
docs.text:
• a thinking engine
  • a content refinement system
  • a foundation for future agent collaboration

docs.title: What This Is Not Yet
docs.text:
• fully autonomous posting
  • multi-platform optimisation
  • long-form content generation

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
• multi-agent orchestration with specialised roles
  • automated insight filtering and prioritisation
  • integration into real-world business workflows
  • low-cost deployment for education and SME use

docs.title: Navigation Inside the Demo
docs.text:
• Agent Identity
  • Workflow Design
  • Troubleshooting Journal
  • Showcase
  • Closing Thought

docs.title: Why It Matters
docs.text: This is one of the early public outputs from the founding cohort. It shows what the community is for: not passive learning, but building visible, testable systems and publishing the thinking behind them.

docs.title: Visit the Demo
docs.text: • Open ZeroClaw Agents

docs.title: Closing Thought
docs.text: This is not just about building an AI tool. It is about creating a system that helps Gemma think better, articulate more clearly, and create consistently.
